claim: The frontier recovers known transcription-factor biology from perturbation alone, with no regulon supplied. Across 154 CollecTRI TFs that are major regulators here, their literature targets are 4.03x enriched among the genes their knockdown moved (Fisher combined p≈1e-26), and when a data edge meets a known one the sign agrees 63% of the time. 19 individual regulons clear significance on their own, and the screen flags 82 known edges where the data overrules the literature's sign.

claim: PGGT1B (geranylgeranyltransferase-I beta subunit) is a stimulation-gated, cell-type-specific regulator of the CD4+ T-cell activation transcriptome, likely acting upstream via prenylation-dependent signaling rather than as a transcription factor.
subject: PGGT1B

evidence.fact: In the frozen screen PGGT1B is classed as a condition_specific_regulator with on-target KD
Near-inert at rest (175 DE) but 3014 DE genes at Stim8hr, i.e. a stimulation-gated effect
Not a canonical T-cell gene, not an activation-module gene, not an essentiality artifact
Cell-type-specific: knockdown is inert in non-immune K562 (1 DE), verdict cell-type-specific
Not an annotated TF and has no known regulon in CollecTRI (0 targets)

verifier.name: frozen-data tools
verifier.method: every fact is a deterministic lookup against a released table; the agent cannot assert ungated biology

scope: It has no annotated regulon and is not a known TF, yet its knockdown reshapes thousands of genes only upon stimulation and only in T cells, pointing to an under-appreciated, likely prenylation-mediated regulatory role rather than a housekeeping or canonical T-cell function.
a hypothesis to test, not an established result
